title: Telosma procumbens
taxonomy: Telosma procumbens is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is a perennial climber native to parts of Asia. The plant is classified as Telosma procumbens in the kingdom Plantae, and it belongs to the family Apocynaceae. The genus Telosma is a group of flowering plants, and Telosma procumbens is one of its species. The classification of Telosma procumbens provides important information about its evolutionary history and relationships to other plants.
uses: Telosma procumbens, also known as the flame vine or coral vine, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, pain, and swelling. In some cultures, Telosma procumbens is used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and stomach pain. Its extracts have also been studied for potential antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Telosma procumbens for medicinal purposes.

intro: Telosma procumbens is a climbing, woody vine native to Southeast Asia, commonly known as the "Butterfly Flower" due to its fragrant, yellow, and star-shaped flowers. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Telosma procumbens include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Telosma procumbens is often prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and is also used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal disorders.
